metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a lightweight proving command for each AC — e.g. `grep -q 'OIDC provider' docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md && grep -q 'sso-auth-host' docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` for AC1; a section-heading grep for each required topic in AC2/AC3 — so completeness is machine-checkable at PR time.
findings.finding: No proving command is specified for any of the three acceptance criteria; an agent could commit the files, self-attest completion, and there would be no deterministic verification step that distinguishes 'file exists with required content' from 'file exists but omits a required section'.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append a 'Verification' block to the plan:
```
# Verification
## AC1
grep -qE 'OIDC provider|wildcard cert|auth_host' docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md
grep -qE 'adr/' docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md
grep -qE 'sso-auth-host' docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md
## AC2 (ADR — locate by newest file under docs/adr/)
ADR=$(ls -t docs/adr/*.md | head -1)
for phrase in 'adopt-vs-teardown' 'import' 'name preservation' 'state migration'; do grep -qi "$phrase" "$ADR" || echo "MISSING: $phrase"; done
## AC3
for phrase in 'auth-host architecture' '/auth-solutions/' 'cookie' 'client_secret' 'revoke'; do grep -qi "$phrase" infra/runbooks/sso-auth-host.md || echo "MISSING: $phrase"; done
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the `sso-auth-host.md` runbook spec what 'Lambda `-replace`' means for cookie-key rotation: specify whether this is `terraform apply -replace=aws_lambda_function.auth_host`, a manual Lambda restart (e.g., `aws lambda update-function-configuration`), or simply relying on the next cold-start after SSM overwrite — so the runbook gives operators an unambiguous, executable command.
findings.finding: The runbook spec names 'Lambda `-replace`' as part of the cookie-signing-key rotation procedure, but this mechanism is not clearly defined. The parent story confirms the Lambda reads the SSM param at runtime with `ignore_changes=[value]`; a simple SSM overwrite should suffice for the next Lambda cold-start, but 'Lambda `-replace`' is ambiguous — it could mean `terraform apply -replace=aws_lambda_function.auth_host` (forced re-creation), a manual Lambda restart/update, or something else. A runbook that misstates the rotation mechanism will mislead an operator during an incident.
findings.location: What section — `infra/runbooks/sso-auth-host.md` spec: 'cookie-signing-key rotation (SSM overwrite + Lambda `-replace`)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the shorthand 'Lambda `-replace`' with the explicit operator action: e.g., 'SSM overwrite (`aws ssm put-parameter --overwrite`) + force a Lambda cold-start (`aws lambda update-function-configuration --function-name snap-demo-auth-host --description <timestamp>` or equivalent) so the new key is loaded on the next invocation'.

findings.finding: The plan states the iam.tf OIDC comment fix was 'already applied in the terraform story's commit,' but the current repo file (infra/terraform/iam.tf:136) still contains the stale text 'The OIDC provider (token.actions.githubusercontent.com) already exists in the account.' The plan's scope section treats this as a pre-landed precondition rather than an open AC item.
findings.location: ## What — 'Fix the stale comment in `infra/terraform/iam.tf`... (already applied in the terraform story's commit)'; also AC: '`infra/terraform/iam.tf`'s OIDC comment no longer claims the provider is externally owned.'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove '(already applied in the terraform story's commit)' from the What section and keep the iam.tf comment fix as a full AC item to be done in this story's commit, since the file still carries the stale text.

findings.finding: The plan states the iam.tf stale comment was 'already applied in the terraform story's commit,' but `infra/terraform/iam.tf:136` still reads 'The OIDC provider (token.actions.githubusercontent.com) already exists in the account.' — the fix is NOT present.
findings.location: What section — 'Fix the stale comment in `infra/terraform/iam.tf`… (already applied in the terraform story's commit)'; also AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '(already applied in the terraform story's commit)' with '(to be applied as part of this ticket)' so the implementer knows the iam.tf edit is still required work.

findings.finding: The plan claims in the 'What' section that the iam.tf comment fix was 'already applied in the terraform story's commit,' but the dependency ticket (ample-famous-cockatiel) only describes rewiring the ARN reference to `aws_iam_openid_connect_provider.github.arn` — it does not explicitly state the stale prose comment at line 136 ('The OIDC provider (token.actions.githubusercontent.com) already exists in the account.') is removed. The comment still exists at HEAD. If the dep ticket doesn't remove that comment text, AC-2 requires this ticket to edit iam.tf, contradicting the plan's 'Documentation only' scope claim.
findings.location: What section: 'Fix the stale comment in infra/terraform/iam.tf… (already applied in the terraform story's commit)'; Scope section: 'Documentation only'; AC-2: 'infra/terraform/iam.tf's OIDC comment no longer claims the provider is externally owned.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to AC 3 a validation clause requiring that at least one operational procedure (e.g., cookie-signing-key rotation or incident revoke) be dry-run or peer-reviewed by a team member before the ticket is closed.
findings.finding: No AC carries a concrete validation mechanism for the runbook's operational accuracy — all three criteria are structural (file exists with named sections) rather than functional (a rotation or revoke procedure was dry-run or peer-walked). A runbook with inaccurate steps is worse than no runbook, so at least one criterion should require evidence that the procedures work.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC 3: 'At least one rotation procedure in the runbook has been verified (dry-run in a non-production context or step-by-step peer walkthrough) and confirmed accurate before merging.'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 'cookie-secret idiom change' to AC2's tradeoffs list so the described ADR content is fully covered by its acceptance criterion.
findings.finding: The 'What' section describes 'cookie-secret idiom change' as a tradeoff recorded in the ADR, but AC2 does not include it as a verification point, leaving a described deliverable detail without a covering criterion.
findings.location: What bullet 2 (ADR description) vs. Acceptance Criteria AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC2 to: 'records the re-homing decision and its tradeoffs (adopt-vs-teardown, import-not-recreate, name preservation, cookie-secret idiom change, state migration).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `auth_origin_secret` (CloudFront custom-origin-header / Lambda `ORIGIN_SECRET` env) to the runbook's secret inventory and specify its rotation procedure (generate new value → update CloudFront origin custom header AND Lambda env atomically, e.g., `aws cloudfront update-distribution` + `aws lambda update-function-configuration --environment`), distinct from the SSM-overwrite path used for `/auth-solutions/*` secrets.
findings.finding: The runbook spec omits the `auth_origin_secret` (CloudFront custom-origin-header ↔ Lambda `ORIGIN_SECRET` env) rotation procedure. This secret is the sole control that prevents direct public bypass of CloudFront to the raw `execute-api` URL. The parent ticket confirms it is live and load-bearing ('Direct execute-api hit (bypassing CloudFront) -> HTTP 403'). The plan's coverage list names only `/auth-solutions/*` (cookie-signing-key + Google client_secret); `auth_origin_secret` lives outside that namespace (in Terraform `random_password` state) and has no rotation path documented in rebar's tree after the ownership transfer.
findings.location: What section — 'Add an SSO operations runbook under infra/runbooks/sso-auth-host.md … covering: … the /auth-solutions/* secrets, cookie-signing-key rotation … Google-client_secret rotation … and the incident revoke lever'; AC3 — 'an infra/runbooks/sso-auth-host.md runbook covers … the /auth-solutions/* secrets, cookie-key + Google-secret rotation, and the incident revoke lever'
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section and AC3, extend the runbook coverage to: 'the auth-host secrets: the two `/auth-solutions/*` SSM params (cookie-signing-key, Google client_secret) AND the CloudFront↔Lambda `ORIGIN_SECRET` (auth_origin_secret), including rotation procedures for each.' For auth_origin_secret specifically, the runbook should note that rotation requires a coordinated dual update: (1) generate a new random value, (2) update the CloudFront distribution's origin custom header, (3) update the Lambda environment variable in the same operation window to avoid a race where one side accepts the old value and the other has already rotated.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly direct the `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` update to remove or qualify the old 'already exists in the account' assumption text (not just add the new subsection alongside it) so the doc is internally consistent.
findings.finding: The plan adds a subsection to `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` asserting rebar ownership of the OIDC provider, but does not explicitly call out removing or correcting the existing text that assumed the OIDC provider 'already exists in the account.' That old assumption text will remain in the doc and conflict with the new ownership subsection, stranding a stale reference within the same file.
findings.location: What section — `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` update; Acceptance Criteria line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Add a plan step and a corresponding AC clause: 'The existing gerrit-aws-setup.md text that treats the OIDC provider as a pre-existing account dependency is updated (or struck) to reflect that rebar now owns and provisions it, so no conflicting assumption remains in the doc.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale for the three-artifact split in the plan's `What` or `Scope` section (e.g., 'ADR for design rationale, runbook for operational procedures, and setup-doc pointer for discoverability — matching the repo's established pattern').
findings.finding: The plan states what three artifacts to produce but offers no positive rationale for why this three-way split (ADR + runbook + gerrit-aws-setup subsection) is the right structure, versus a single comprehensive document or an alternative decomposition.
findings.location: What section / Scope section — overall plan structure

findings.finding: AC2 (`infra/terraform/iam.tf` comment fix) is described in the 'What' section as 'already applied in the terraform story's commit', yet it appears as a standalone acceptance criterion in this ticket with no dependency edge to story ample-famous-cockatiel. An executor cannot tell whether (a) they must make the change here, (b) they must verify a sibling commit, or (c) the AC is already satisfied and merely needs attestation — and there is no stated proving command or cross-ticket dependency link.
findings.location: What section ('Fix the stale comment in `infra/terraform/iam.tf`') and Acceptance Criteria AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dependency note: 'Depends on ample-famous-cockatiel merging first. AC2 proving command: `grep -c "already exists in the account" infra/terraform/iam.tf` must return 0.'

findings.finding: The leaf claims `infra/terraform/iam.tf`'s stale OIDC comment as one of its four named artifacts (What section) and backs it with a dedicated AC, even though (a) the parent story (ample-famous-cockatiel) explicitly owns `infra/terraform/iam.tf` in its file_impact list and delivers that fix in its own commit, (b) the parent's delegated scope for this leaf is documentation only ("Docs are child 6725"), and (c) the leaf's own What section admits the fix was "already applied in the terraform story's commit." Including a terraform file as an AC in a docs-only leaf re-defines a deliverable the parent owns, creating ambiguous dual-ownership and blurring the leaf's declared scope boundary.
findings.location: Leaf plan: What bullet 2 ("Fix the stale comment in `infra/terraform/iam.tf`") and AC line 2 ("`infra/terraform/iam.tf`'s OIDC comment no longer claims the provider is externally owned")
findings.suggested_fix: Delete the second What bullet ("Fix the stale comment in `infra/terraform/iam.tf`…") and the second AC line ("`infra/terraform/iam.tf`'s OIDC comment no longer claims…") from the leaf. Add a brief note under Prerequisites or Context: "The stale iam.tf comment is resolved in the companion terraform story (ample-famous-cockatiel); this leaf verifies final prose only."

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
